question: An ad-hoc sub-process is handled internally by Zeebe with `completionCondition` set as shown and `cancelRemainingInstances` set to `false`. Five inner activities have been activated; the third one completes and the condition evaluates to `true`. What happens to the two activities still running?

options.text: They are terminated immediately and the ad-hoc sub-process completes right away.
They keep running, and the ad-hoc sub-process completes only after both remaining instances finish.
An incident is raised because `cancelRemainingInstances` must be `true` when a `completionCondition` is defined.
They are paused until a future inner-element completion re-evaluates `completionCondition`.

a.text: Immediate termination of remaining instances is the behavior when `cancelRemainingInstances` is `true` (the default), not when it is set to `false`.

b.text: When `cancelRemainingInstances` is `false`, the ad-hoc sub-process waits for the completion of all active instances before completing, even after the `completionCondition` has been met.

c.text: Both `true` and `false` are valid settings for `cancelRemainingInstances`; setting it to `false` does not cause an incident.

d.text: There is no pause-and-re-evaluate behavior. Once the condition is true, the sub-process either cancels the remaining instances or waits for them, depending on `cancelRemainingInstances`.

explanation: An ad-hoc sub-process evaluates its `completionCondition` after every inner element completes. When the condition becomes true, the `cancelRemainingInstances` attribute decides what happens to still-active inner instances: `true` (the default) terminates them and completes the sub-process immediately; `false` waits for all active instances to complete before the sub-process completes.
